The previous action was TURN_LEFT, which changed the yaw from 60 to 30 degrees. The bearing changed from +170 to -160 degrees. This indicates that turning left crossed the 180/-180 boundary, flipping the sign of the bearing back to negative. The goal is now to the right (negative bearing).
The bearing is in the "behind_right" category. The learned skills for "behind_right & far" indicate that MOVE_FORWARD is bad (regression) and TURN_LEFT is bad (turning away). The correct action is to turn towards the goal, which is to the right.
Therefore, TURN_RIGHT is the correct action to align with the goal.

TURN_RIGHT

text:
The goal bearing is +170 degrees (behind_left). The distance is 12434 cm (far).
The previous action was TURN_RIGHT, which changed the yaw from 30 to 60 degrees. The bearing changed from -160 to +170 degrees. This indicates that turning right crossed the 180/-180 boundary, flipping the sign of the bearing. The goal is now to the left (positive bearing).
The bearing is in the "behind_left" category. The learned skills for "behind_left & far" indicate that MOVE_FORWARD is bad (regression) and TURN_RIGHT is bad (turning away). The correct action is to turn towards the goal, which is to the left.
Therefore, TURN_LEFT is the correct action to align with the goal.

TURN_LEFT
